markdown.markdown:
I am currently a fifth-year PhD student in Agricultural and Resource Economics at [Colorado State University](https://www.colostate.edu/). I plan to complete my preliminary dissertation defense in Fall 2026 and my final dissertation defense in Fall 2027.

Prior to joining Colorado State University, I earned a Master’s degree in Economics from the [Institute of Business Administration (IBA), Karachi](https://www.iba.edu.pk/) in Pakistan. My graduate training focused on econometrics, development economics, and international trade. I completed my undergraduate studies in Economics at [Quaid-i-Azam University](https://qau.edu.pk/), Islamabad, Pakistan.

During my PhD at Colorado State University, I have been involved in research assistantship roles. I was part of an interdisciplinary research project examining subsurface drip irrigation efficiency and deficit irrigation strategies for alfalfa production in Colorado, the [Subsurface Irrigation Efficiency Project (SIEP)](https://www.siepwater.com/). The project evaluated how irrigation management practices influence agricultural water use efficiency and production outcomes under water scarcity conditions.


My research interests are in applied microeconomics, environmental and resource economics, agricultural conservation policies, climate risk, flood damages, and crop insurance.

My current research primarily examines the causal effects of cover crop adoption on crop insurance losses and planting failure risk in U.S. agriculture. More broadly, my work studies how agricultural conservation practices influence watershed hydrology, runoff generation, and downstream flood damages. I am particularly interested in understanding the potential environmental externalities and risk-mitigation benefits associated with conservation agriculture under increasing climate variability.

---
